info.title: JustHereToListen.io Admin API
info.description:
A **multi-tenant meeting bot API** service. Send bots into **Zoom**, **Google Meet**, **Microsoft Teams**, and **onepizza.io** meetings to record, transcribe, and analyse them with **Claude** (Anthropic) or **Gemini** (Google) AI.

## How it works
1. Register an account (email/password or Google/Microsoft SSO) → receive an `sk_live_...` API key
2. Top up credits via **Stripe card** or **USDC (ERC-20)**
3. `POST /api/v1/bot` with your `meeting_url` and optional `webhook_url`
4. A headless Chromium bot joins the meeting, records audio (and optionally video), and transcribes it
5. Results are POSTed to your `webhook_url` when done (or poll `GET /api/v1/bot/{id}`)
6. Results persist in the database — browse past meetings via the **Meeting History** tab in the dashboard

## Authentication
All API calls (except `/api/v1/auth/register` and `/api/v1/auth/login`) require:
```
Authorization: Bearer sk_live_<your-api-key>
```
API keys are prefixed with `sk_live_` and shown **once** at creation — copy immediately. The legacy `API_KEY` environment variable acts as a superadmin bypass and skips per-user account checks. Leave it unset to enforce per-user auth.

## Accounts & API keys
Register at `POST /api/v1/auth/register` to receive your first `sk_live_...` key. Generate additional named keys with `POST /api/v1/auth/keys`. Revoke individual keys with `DELETE /api/v1/auth/keys/{id}`.

**Account types:** Pass `account_type: "personal"` (default) or `"business"` at registration. See the **Business accounts** section below.

**USDC wallet:** Register your Ethereum wallet with `PUT /api/v1/auth/wallet` so the platform can automatically attribute USDC deposits to your account.

**Notification preferences:** Configure email alerts with `GET/PUT /api/v1/auth/notify`. Enable `notify_on_done` to receive an email when each bot finishes analysis.

**Subscription plan:** View your plan and monthly usage with `GET /api/v1/auth/plan`. Plans: `free` (5 bots/mo), `starter` (50), `pro` (500), `business` (unlimited).

**GDPR erasure:** Permanently delete your account and all data with `DELETE /api/v1/auth/account`.

## SSO — Google & Microsoft OAuth2
Sign in or register with an existing Google or Microsoft account (when configured by the platform admin via `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`/`GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET` or `MICROSOFT_CLIENT_ID`/`MICROSOFT_CLIENT_SECRET`).

- `GET /api/v1/auth/oauth/{provider}/authorize` — redirect to provider login (`provider`: `google` or `microsoft`). Pass `?redirect=1` to use the cookie flow for the web UI.
- `GET /api/v1/auth/oauth/{provider}/callback` — OAuth2 callback; returns `{account_id, email, api_key, access_token, is_new_account}`.
On first login the response includes a new `sk_live_...` API key. Subsequent logins return an empty `api_key` — use your existing key.

## Business accounts (multi-user data isolation)
Business accounts are for **platforms integrating JustHereToListen.io on behalf of multiple end-users**. A single business account uses one API key and one shared credit balance, but isolates all bot data between end-users.

**How to use:** Pass the `X-Sub-User: <user-id>` header on every request to scope data to a specific end-user. Users with different sub-user IDs cannot see each other's bots, transcripts, or analyses. Omit the header for an account-wide view of all bots.

**Alternatively**, pass `sub_user_id` in the `POST /api/v1/bot` request body — the body field takes precedence over the header.

`X-Sub-User` is an opaque string (max 255 chars): user ID, email, UUID, etc.

## Credits & billing
Credits are deducted per bot run. Default: `BOT_FLAT_FEE_USD` = $0.10 flat fee per bot. When flat fee is disabled (`BOT_FLAT_FEE_USD=0`), billing uses raw AI cost × `CREDIT_MARKUP` (default 3×). A minimum balance of `MIN_CREDITS_USD` (default $0.10) is required to create a bot (HTTP 402 if below this threshold).

**Top up via Stripe card:** `POST /api/v1/billing/stripe/checkout` — returns a Stripe Checkout URL. Credits are applied automatically once payment is confirmed via the Stripe webhook. Valid amounts are set by `STRIPE_TOP_UP_AMOUNTS` (default: 10, 25, 50, 100 USD).

**Top up via USDC (ERC-20):** First register your Ethereum wallet via `PUT /api/v1/auth/wallet`. Then `GET /api/v1/billing/usdc/address` returns the platform deposit address. Send USDC **from your registered wallet** — the system matches the `from` address to your account. 1 USDC = $1 credit, credited automatically within ~1 minute of on-chain confirmation.

**Subscribe to a plan:** `POST /api/v1/billing/subscribe` — create a Stripe Checkout session for a subscription plan (`starter`, `pro`, or `business`). Returns `{session_id, checkout_url, plan}`. Pass optional `success_url` and `cancel_url`.

**Check balance:** `GET /api/v1/billing/balance` — returns current `credits_usd` and the last 50 transactions. Transaction `type` values: `stripe_topup`, `usdc_topup`, `bot_usage`.

## Webhooks (global, with retry & delivery logs)
Register a global webhook with `POST /api/v1/webhook` to receive events for **all** bots. For per-bot webhooks, pass `webhook_url` when creating a bot instead.

**Events:** `bot.joining`, `bot.in_call`, `bot.call_ended`, `bot.transcript_ready`, `bot.analysis_ready`, `bot.done`, `bot.error`, `bot.cancelled`. Use `events: ["*"]` to receive all (default).

**Retry logic:** Failed deliveries are retried up to `WEBHOOK_MAX_ATTEMPTS` times (default 5) with exponential backoff: 1 min, 5 min, 25 min, 2 h, 10 h.

**Signatures:** Set a `secret` when registering — each delivery includes an `X-MeetingBot-Signature: sha256=<hmac>` header for verification.

**Delivery logs:** `GET /api/v1/webhook/{id}/deliveries` — paginated delivery history with status, HTTP response code, error message, and next retry time.

**Test endpoint:** `POST /api/v1/webhook/{id}/test` — send a test event immediately.

## Integrations (Slack & Notion)
Push meeting notes automatically to third-party tools after each bot session.

- `POST /api/v1/integrations` — create an integration (`type`: `slack` or `notion`)
- **Slack:** provide `config.webhook_url` (Incoming Webhook URL)
- **Notion:** provide `config.api_token` and `config.database_id`
- `GET /api/v1/integrations` — list all integrations (secrets redacted)
- `PATCH /api/v1/integrations/{id}` — update config
- `DELETE /api/v1/integrations/{id}` — remove integration

After bot analysis completes, summaries and action items are pushed to all active integrations for that account.

## Calendar auto-join
Connect an iCal feed so JustHereToListen.io automatically dispatches bots to upcoming meetings.

- `POST /api/v1/calendar` — add an iCal feed (`ical_url`, `name`, `bot_name?`, `auto_record`)
- `GET /api/v1/calendar` — list all calendar feeds
- `PATCH /api/v1/calendar/{id}` — update a feed
- `DELETE /api/v1/calendar/{id}` — remove a feed
- `POST /api/v1/calendar/{id}/sync` — manually trigger an immediate sync

The background service polls all active feeds every `CALENDAR_POLL_INTERVAL_S` seconds (default 5 min) and auto-creates bots for meetings starting within the next 10 minutes.

## Idempotency keys
Prevent duplicate bots from network retries by passing a unique key on bot creation:
```
Idempotency-Key: <your-unique-key>
```
If a second request with the same key arrives within `IDEMPOTENCY_TTL_HOURS` (default 24 h), the original bot is returned instead of creating a new one. Keys are scoped to your account.

## Bot persona
Customize how the bot appears in meetings with `bot_name` and `bot_avatar_url` fields on `POST /api/v1/bot`. The platform default avatar is set via `DEFAULT_BOT_AVATAR_URL`.

## Video recording
When `VIDEO_RECORDING_ENABLED=true` (default), the bot captures a screen recording alongside audio. Video is encoded with ffmpeg (configurable `VIDEO_CRF`, `VIDEO_FPS`, `VIDEO_SCALE`). Download the recording via `GET /api/v1/bot/{id}/recording`.

## Cloud storage
Set `STORAGE_BACKEND=s3` to upload recordings to S3-compatible storage (AWS S3, Cloudflare R2, MinIO). Configure with `S3_BUCKET`, `S3_ENDPOINT_URL`, `S3_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `S3_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`. Set `S3_PUBLIC_URL` for CDN-served links.
Default: `local` — recordings are stored on disk with automatic cleanup after `RECORDING_RETENTION_DAYS` days.

## Consent & recording announcement
Set `consent_enabled: true` on bot creation to announce recording at join. Transcripts are scanned for the opt-out phrase — opted-out participants' content is redacted. Configure globally via `CONSENT_ANNOUNCEMENT_ENABLED` and `CONSENT_OPT_OUT_PHRASE` env vars.

## Data retention policies
`GET/PUT /api/v1/retention` — configure per-account retention days for bots, recordings, and transcripts. Use `-1` for keep-forever. Platform defaults: `DEFAULT_BOT_RETENTION_DAYS` (90 days), `DEFAULT_RECORDING_RETENTION_DAYS` (30 days). A background task enforces policies nightly.

## Keyword alerts
`POST /api/v1/keyword-alerts` — register a keyword. When detected in a completed transcript a `bot.keyword_alert` webhook event fires. Set per-bot via `keyword_alerts: [{keyword, webhook_url?}]` at creation.

## Workspaces
`POST /api/v1/workspaces` — create a shared workspace. Invite members with roles (`admin` / `member` / `viewer`). Tag bots with `workspace_id` to share with workspace members. Enable/disable with `WORKSPACES_ENABLED` (default `true`).

## MCP (Model Context Protocol)
`GET /api/v1/mcp/schema` returns the server manifest. `POST /api/v1/mcp/call` executes tools: `list_meetings`, `get_meeting`, `search_meetings`, `get_action_items`, `get_meeting_brief`. Enable/disable with `MCP_ENABLED` (default `true`).

## SAML 2.0 SSO
Set `SAML_ENABLED=true` and `SAML_SP_BASE_URL`. Admins register IdP configs at `POST /api/v1/auth/saml/configs`. Users authenticate at `GET /api/v1/auth/saml/{org_slug}/authorize`.

## Exports
Export meeting reports in multiple formats:
- `GET /api/v1/bot/{id}/export/markdown` — Markdown report with transcript, summary, and action items
- `GET /api/v1/bot/{id}/export/pdf` — PDF report (same content)
- `GET /api/v1/bot/{id}/export/json` — Full session as structured JSON
- `GET /api/v1/bot/{id}/export/srt` — Transcript as SRT subtitle file

## Analytics
`GET /api/v1/analytics` — platform-wide bot statistics including sentiment distribution, meetings per day, top topics, top participants, and platform breakdown.

`GET /api/v1/analytics/usage` — per-account usage dashboard: `bots_used`, `bots_limit`, `plan`, `credits_balance`, `credits_spent_this_month`, `avg_cost_per_bot`, `billing_cycle_reset`, and `daily_usage[]` (last 30 days).

`GET /api/v1/analytics/trends?days=30` — longitudinal meeting trends: `total_meetings`, `total_hours`, `meetings_per_day[]`, `sentiment_trend[]`, `health_trend[]`, `top_topics[]`, `cost_trend[]`.

## Analysis templates
Use predefined templates to customize analysis output:
- `GET /api/v1/templates` — list all templates (default, sales, standup, 1:1, retro, etc.)
- Pass `template: "<seed>"` when creating a bot, or `prompt_override` for a one-off prompt
- `GET /api/v1/templates/default-prompt` — get the raw default analysis prompt

## Admin
Admin endpoints are restricted to accounts in `ADMIN_EMAILS` (env var) or with `is_admin=true` in the database. All others receive HTTP 403. Full interactive docs at `GET /api/v1/admin/docs`.

**USDC & billing:**
- `GET/PUT /api/v1/admin/wallet` — view or set the platform USDC collection wallet
- `GET/PUT /api/v1/admin/rpc-url` — view or set the Ethereum RPC URL (no restart needed)
- `GET /api/v1/admin/config` — list all platform configuration values
- `POST /api/v1/admin/credit` — manually credit a user account
- `GET /api/v1/admin/usdc/unmatched` — list USDC transfers that couldn't be attributed
- `POST /api/v1/admin/usdc/unmatched/{tx_hash}/resolve` — mark unmatched transfer resolved
- `POST /api/v1/admin/usdc/rescan` — reset USDC monitor block pointer for rescan

**Account management:**
- `POST /api/v1/admin/accounts/{account_id}/set-account-type` — change any account's type
- `POST /api/v1/auth/saml/configs` — register SAML IdP config (admin only)

**Admin web UI** at `/admin` provides:
- Platform stats (accounts, credits, revenue, unmatched transfers)
- Subscription plan breakdown (Free/Starter/Pro/Business counts)
- Bot activity stats + platform feature counters (webhooks, integrations, calendar feeds, SSO)
- System status indicators (Stripe, RPC, HD seed, Email, Storage, Video, Google SSO, Microsoft SSO, SAML, Whisper)
- Inline plan management — change any account's plan via a dropdown
- Monthly bot usage per account
- Manual credit form, USDC rescan, wallet & RPC configuration
- Unmatched USDC transfer resolution
- User account enable/disable/admin-toggle/account-type-switch

## Prometheus metrics
`GET /metrics` — Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint (unauthenticated). Includes HTTP request counts/latencies, active bot counts, and billing totals.

## Bot response & analysis
Bot responses include: `id`, `status`, `meeting_platform`, `participants`, `transcript` (`[{speaker, text, timestamp}]`), `analysis` (`{summary, key_points, action_items, decisions, next_steps, sentiment, topics}`), `chapters` (`[{title, start_time, summary}]`), `speaker_stats` (`[{name, talk_time_s, talk_pct, turns}]`), `recording_available`, `is_demo_transcript`, `sub_user_id`, `metadata`, and `ai_usage` (`{total_tokens, total_cost_usd, primary_model, operations: [{operation, provider, model, input_tokens, output_tokens, total_tokens, cost_usd, duration_s}]}`).

## AI providers
Set `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` for Claude (preferred) or `GEMINI_API_KEY` for Gemini. When both are set, Claude takes precedence for transcription and analysis.

## Bot lifecycle
`ready` / `scheduled` / `queued` → `joining` → `in_call` → `call_ended` → `transcribing` → `done` (or `error` / `cancelled`)

- **`scheduled`** — bot has a future `join_at` time and is waiting
- **`queued`** — `MAX_CONCURRENT_BOTS` limit reached; waiting for a free slot
- **`done`** — transcript + analysis complete; results available for 24 hours

## Rate limits
- `POST /api/v1/auth/register` — 3 requests/min per IP
- `POST /api/v1/auth/login` — 5 requests/min per IP
- `POST /api/v1/bot` — 20 requests/min per IP

Exceeded limits return HTTP 429.

## Real-time events (WebSocket)
Connect to `ws://<host>/api/v1/ws` for live bot status updates. The server broadcasts events whenever a bot's status changes.

## Auto-leave
The bot leaves automatically when it has been the only participant for `BOT_ALONE_TIMEOUT` seconds (default 5 min).

## Web UI
| Path | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `/register` | Create account (Personal or Business); Google/Microsoft SSO sign-up |
| `/login` | Login with email/password or SSO |
| `/dashboard` | Balance, API keys, plan & usage, notification prefs, USDC wallet, linked SSO accounts, integrations summary, calendar feeds, transactions |
| `/topup` | Add credits via Stripe card or USDC |
| `/bot/{id}` | Session viewer — transcript, AI analysis (summary, key points, action items, decisions, next steps, sentiment, topics), speaker breakdown, chapters, meeting metadata, and download links for audio/video/markdown/PDF |
| `/admin` | Platform admin — plan stats, bot activity, system status, user accounts with inline plan management, unmatched USDC transfers, manual credit |

post.description: Create a new account and return the first API key.

Authenticate and receive a JWT for web UI sessions.

**Request format:** `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` (OAuth2 password flow).
Send `username` (your email) and `password` as form fields — **not** JSON.

The returned JWT is only for the browser-based web UI (`/dashboard`, `/topup`).
For API calls, use your `sk_live_...` API key with `Authorization: Bearer <key>`.

post.description:
Generate a sandbox API key (`sk_test_...`).

Test keys behave like live keys except:
- Bot creation returns a mock bot with a demo transcript immediately.
- Credits are **not** deducted.
- Useful for local development and CI/CD pipelines.

The full key is returned once — store it securely.

put.summary: Switch between personal and business account
put.description:
Switch the current account between `personal` and `business` types.

- **personal** — standard single-user account (default).
- **business** — multi-tenant mode; pass `X-Sub-User: <id>` on every request
  to isolate bots, transcripts, and analyses per end-user.

Switching type does **not** affect existing bot data or credits.

delete.description:
Permanently delete the current account and all associated data (GDPR right to erasure).

This action is **irreversible**.  It deletes:
- The account and all API keys
- All bot snapshots for this account
- All calendar feeds and integrations
- All billing records (Stripe top-ups, USDC deposits, credit transactions)
- All cloud recordings (if S3 storage is configured)

An audit log entry is written before deletion so the action is traceable.

Generate a support access key.

The plaintext key is returned **once** and never stored — only its SHA-256
hash is persisted. Share the plaintext key with support during a session,
then revoke it immediately after. The admin cannot recover the key from the
database.

get.description:
Redirect the user to the OAuth2 provider login page.

After the user grants consent, the provider redirects to
`GET /api/v1/auth/oauth/{provider}/callback`.

Pass `?redirect=1` if you want the callback to set a session cookie and
redirect to the web UI instead of returning JSON.

schema.description: Set to '1' to redirect back to the web UI with a session cookie after login.

get.description:
Handle the OAuth2 callback from the provider.

On success returns:
```json
{
  "account_id": "...",
  "email": "...",
  "api_key": "sk_live_...",
  "access_token": "<JWT for web UI>",
  "token_type": "bearer",
  "is_new_account": true
}
```

`api_key` is the `sk_live_...` key for API calls.
`access_token` is a short-lived JWT for the web UI (same as `POST /auth/login`).
On subsequent logins `api_key` will be empty — use your existing key.

post.summary: Create a Stripe Checkout session for a top-up
post.description:
Create a Stripe Checkout session to top up your credit balance via card payment.

`amount_usd` must be one of the values in `STRIPE_TOP_UP_AMOUNTS` (default: 10, 25, 50, 100).

Returns a `checkout_url` — redirect your user to that URL to complete payment.
After a successful payment, credits are added to your balance automatically via
the Stripe webhook (`POST /api/v1/billing/stripe/webhook`).

Optional `success_url` and `cancel_url` override the default redirect destinations.

get.description:
Get the USDC deposit address and instructions.

**Platform wallet mode** (preferred): The admin sets a single collection wallet.
You must first register your own Ethereum wallet via `PUT /api/v1/auth/wallet`
so the system can match incoming transfers to your account by the `from` address.

**HD wallet mode** (legacy fallback): If no platform wallet is configured,
each user gets a unique deposit address derived from an HD seed.

1 USDC = $1.00 credit. Credits are added automatically within ~1 minute.

post.description:
Create a new meeting bot.

The bot joins the meeting, records audio, transcribes with Gemini/Claude,
and delivers results to your `webhook_url` when done.

Poll `GET /api/v1/bot/{id}` until `status` is `done` (or `error`).

**Business accounts:** Pass `sub_user_id` in the body or the `X-Sub-User` header
to scope this bot to a specific end-user. When set, only requests with the same
sub-user identifier can access this bot's data.

**Idempotency:** Supply an `Idempotency-Key` header to safely retry the request.
A second call with the same key returns the original bot (with header
`X-Idempotency-Replayed: true`) instead of creating a duplicate.

**Platforms supported for real recording:** Google Meet, Zoom, Microsoft Teams.
Other platforms run in demo mode (AI-generated sample transcript).

For per-bot webhooks (results from a single bot), pass `webhook_url` when

get.description:
Cross-meeting full-text search across transcripts.

Searches both the in-memory 24-hour window AND persisted bot snapshots in the
database when `include_archived=true` (the default).

With `semantic=false` (default): fast substring search across all transcripts.
With `semantic=true`: embed the query and rank meetings by cosine similarity
against stored `summary_embedding` vectors (falls back to substring if no embeddings).

Returns up to `limit` transcript snippets whose text contains the query
string (case-insensitive), each annotated with its source bot context.
Results are ordered by bot creation time (newest first).

parameters.name: q

schema.description: Search query — matched case-insensitively against transcript text.
schema.title: Q

parameters.description: Search query — matched case-insensitively against transcript text.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of matching snippets to return.
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of matching snippets to return.

parameters.name: include_archived

schema.description: When true (default), also searches archived bot snapshots in the database beyond the 24-hour in-memory window. Set false to search only active/recent bots.

get.description:
List USDC transfers that arrived at the platform wallet but could not be
attributed to any user account because the sender's wallet was not registered.

These represent funds that were received on-chain but have not yet been credited
to any user balance. To resolve:

1. Identify the user by their `from_address` (ask them which wallet they used).
2. Credit their account via `POST /admin/credit`.
3. Mark the transfer resolved via `POST /admin/usdc/unmatched/{tx_hash}/resolve`.

Use `?resolved=false` (default) to see only pending items, `?resolved=true` for
resolved ones, or omit the parameter to see all.

post.description:
Reset the USDC monitor's last-processed block so it will rescan from a
given block on its next cycle.

Use this when a USDC deposit was missed because the monitor had already
advanced past that block (e.g. the user sent from an unregistered wallet
and later registered it).  After calling this endpoint the monitor will
re-examine all Transfer events starting at `from_block` on the next 60-second
tick; duplicate transactions are skipped automatically via idempotency checks.

credits_usd.title: Credits Usd
credits_usd.description: Current prepaid credit balance in USD.

wallet_address.title: Wallet Address
wallet_address.description: Your registered Ethereum wallet address for USDC deposits. Set this so the platform can automatically attribute USDC transfers to your account.

is_active.title: Is Active
is_active.description: False if the account has been disabled by an admin.

account_type.title: Account Type
account_type.description: Account type: `personal` for individual users, or `business` for platforms integrating JustHereToListen.io on behalf of multiple end-users. Business accounts can pass `X-Sub-User` to isolate data per end-user.

instruction.title: Instruction
instruction.description: Natural-language directive, e.g. 'Ask about Q2 timeline', 'Push back if scope creeps', 'Summarise every 10 minutes'.

trigger.title: Trigger
trigger.description: When the bot should evaluate this instruction. `on_topic` — when the relevant topic comes up. `on_silence` — after N seconds of silence. `on_interval` — every N seconds. `manual` — only when triggered via the API.

interval_seconds.title: Interval Seconds
interval_seconds.description: For trigger=on_interval / on_silence — seconds between evaluations.

speak.title: Speak
speak.description: When true, the bot uses TTS to speak the response. When false, it posts in chat only.

max_invocations.title: Max Invocations
max_invocations.description: Cap on how many times this single instruction can fire during the meeting.

AgenticInstruction.title: AgenticInstruction
AgenticInstruction.description: A single delegated instruction the bot should attempt to act on during a meeting.

id.title: Id
id.description: Unique key UUID (used to revoke the key).

key_preview.title: Key Preview
key_preview.description: First 16 characters of the key followed by `...` — the full key is only shown once at creation.

mode.title: Mode
mode.description: `live` for production keys, `test` for sandbox keys that return demo data without deducting credits.

is_active.title: Is Active
is_active.description: False if the key has been revoked.

last_used_at.title: Last Used At
last_used_at.description: Last time this key was used for an authenticated request (UTC), or null if never used.

question.title: Question
question.description: Free-form question about the meeting transcript.

credits_usd.title: Credits Usd
credits_usd.description: Current prepaid credit balance in USD.

transactions.title: Transactions
transactions.description: Last 50 transactions ordered by most recent first.

meeting_url.title: Meeting Url
meeting_url.description: Full URL of the Zoom, Google Meet, Teams, or onepizza.io meeting.

bot_name.title: Bot Name
bot_name.description: Display name shown in the meeting.

webhook_url.title: Webhook Url
webhook_url.description: HTTPS URL to POST the full meeting results to once the bot finishes. The payload includes status, transcript, analysis, participants, and AI usage. Leave empty if you prefer to poll GET /api/v1/bot/{id} instead.

join_at.title: Join At
join_at.description: ISO-8601 datetime to schedule the bot join. Omit to join immediately.

analysis_mode.title: Analysis Mode
analysis_mode.description: `full` — AI summary, key points, action items, decisions, sentiment, topics, chapters. `transcript_only` — skip all AI analysis, return only the raw transcript.

template.title: Template
template.description: Built-in analysis template. One of: default, sales, standup, 1on1, retro, kickoff, allhands, postmortem, interview, design-review. Leave empty for the default general-purpose template.

prompt_override.title: Prompt Override
prompt_override.description: Custom analysis prompt. Overrides `template` when both are provided.

vocabulary.title: Vocabulary
vocabulary.description: Domain-specific terms to improve transcription accuracy (product names, jargon, etc.). Max 100 terms.

respond_on_mention.title: Respond On Mention
respond_on_mention.description: When true, the bot monitors live captions and replies when its name is mentioned.

tts_provider.title: Tts Provider
tts_provider.description: `edge` — Microsoft Edge TTS (fast, free). `gemini` — Google Gemini TTS (more natural).

live_transcription.title: Live Transcription
live_transcription.description: Transcribe audio in real-time during the call (15-second chunks).

sub_user_id.title: Sub User Id
sub_user_id.description: For business accounts: an opaque identifier for the end-user this bot belongs to. When set, only requests with the same sub_user_id (via this field or X-Sub-User header) can see this bot's data. Can also be set via the X-Sub-User request header.

bot_avatar_url.title: Bot Avatar Url
bot_avatar_url.description: URL of the avatar image shown as the bot's profile picture in the meeting.

record_video.title: Record Video
record_video.description: Capture a video recording of the meeting screen (MP4) in addition to audio. Download via GET /api/v1/bot/{id}/video once status is `done`.

consent_enabled.title: Consent Enabled
consent_enabled.description: When true, the bot announces the recording at the start of the meeting and monitors for opt-out requests. Participants who say or type the opt-out phrase are removed from the transcript.

consent_message.title: Consent Message
consent_message.description: Custom consent announcement text. Overrides the platform default when set.

keyword_alerts.title: Keyword Alerts
keyword_alerts.description: List of keyword/phrase triggers (max 50). When any keyword is detected in the transcript, a `bot.keyword_alert` webhook event is fired. Account-level KeywordAlert rules defined at /api/v1/keyword-alerts are also applied automatically.

auto_followup_email.title: Auto Followup Email
auto_followup_email.description: When true, automatically generate and send a follow-up email draft to the account's notification email after the meeting analysis is complete.

workspace_id.title: Workspace Id
workspace_id.description: Associate this bot with a team workspace (see /api/v1/workspaces).

transcription_provider.title: Transcription Provider
transcription_provider.description: `gemini` — Gemini Files API (default, requires GEMINI_API_KEY). `whisper` — local OpenAI Whisper model (privacy-preserving, requires WHISPER_ENABLED=true).

translation_language.title: Translation Language
translation_language.description: BCP-47 language code for real-time translation of live transcript entries (e.g. `es` for Spanish, `fr` for French). When set, each live entry is also broadcast as a `bot.live_transcript_translated` WebSocket event.

pii_redaction.title: Pii Redaction
pii_redaction.description: When true, detect and redact PII (emails, phone numbers, SSNs, credit card numbers) from the transcript before analysis. Redacted text is replaced with `[REDACTED]`.

avg_hourly_rate_usd.title: Avg Hourly Rate Usd
avg_hourly_rate_usd.description: Average attendee hourly rate in USD. When provided, `meeting_cost_usd` is calculated as `attendee_count × rate × duration_hours` and included in the bot response.

enable_chat_qa.title: Enable Chat Qa
enable_chat_qa.description: When true, the bot watches the in-meeting chat for messages starting with the configured trigger (default `@bot`) and replies inline using the live transcript as context. Off by default.

chat_qa.description: Fine-tunes how chat-Q&A is triggered and answered. Ignored when `enable_chat_qa` is false.

enable_speaker_analytics.title: Enable Speaker Analytics
enable_speaker_analytics.description: When true, periodically compute and emit per-speaker talk-time, interruption count, and (optionally) sentiment via SSE/WS. Off by default.

speaker_analytics.description: Snapshot interval and which signals to compute. Ignored when `enable_speaker_analytics` is false.

enable_decision_detection.title: Enable Decision Detection
enable_decision_detection.description: When true, detect decision and action moments in real time and fire `bot.decision_detected` webhook events with timestamp + speaker. Off by default.

enable_cross_meeting_memory.title: Enable Cross Meeting Memory
enable_cross_meeting_memory.description: When true, retrieve summaries of semantically related past meetings and (optionally) inject them into this meeting's analysis prompt. Off by default.

cross_meeting_memory.description: Lookback window, scope, and injection toggle. Ignored when `enable_cross_meeting_memory` is false.

enable_coaching.title: Enable Coaching
enable_coaching.description: When true, run a private coaching engine that emits tips to the host (talk-time dominance, filler words, monologue length, etc.). Tips are streamed over a private SSE channel by default. Off by default.

coaching.description: Which signals to track and how to deliver tips. Ignored when `enable_coaching` is false.

agentic_instructions.title: Agentic Instructions
agentic_instructions.description: Natural-language directives for the bot to act on during the meeting. Empty list = standard listener-only behaviour. Max 20 instructions.

agentic_autonomy.title: Agentic Autonomy
agentic_autonomy.description: Master autonomy switch. `off` ignores `agentic_instructions`. `low` only acts on `manual` triggers, `medium` adds `on_topic`, `high` allows `on_silence` and `on_interval` triggers too.

metadata.title: Metadata
metadata.description: Arbitrary key-value pairs stored with the bot and echoed in all responses. Max 20 keys, 64 char keys, 256 char string values.

BotCreate.title: BotCreate
BotCreate.description: Request body for creating a new meeting bot.

status.title: Status
status.description:
Current lifecycle status:
- `ready` — created, about to join immediately
- `scheduled` — waiting for the scheduled `join_at` time
- `queued` — waiting for a free bot slot (`MAX_CONCURRENT_BOTS` reached)
- `joining` — Chromium browser launching and joining the meeting
- `in_call` — recording in progress
- `call_ended` — meeting ended, audio saved
- `transcribing` — audio being transcribed by AI
- `done` — transcript and analysis complete ✓
- `error` — unrecoverable error (see `error_message`)
- `cancelled` — stopped via DELETE /api/v1/bot/{id}

Poll this endpoint until `done` or `error`, or use `webhook_url` for push delivery.

transcript.title: Transcript
transcript.description: Array of `{speaker, text, timestamp, source, message_id?}` entries. `source` is `voice` for spoken utterances (default) or `chat` for messages captured from the meeting chat panel. `message_id` is a short stable hash used internally for chat dedup. Available once status is `done`.

analysis.description: AI-generated analysis. Available once status is `done` (analysis_mode=full).

recording_available.title: Recording Available
recording_available.description: True when a WAV recording can be downloaded via GET /api/v1/bot/{id}/recording.

video_available.title: Video Available
video_available.description: True when an MP4 video recording can be downloaded via GET /api/v1/bot/{id}/video.

bot_avatar_url.title: Bot Avatar Url
bot_avatar_url.description: Bot avatar URL used in the meeting.

translation_language.title: Translation Language
translation_language.description: BCP-47 language the stored transcript was translated to (if post-meeting translation was applied).

health_score.title: Health Score
health_score.description: Meeting quality score from 0–100, computed from participation balance, decision count, action item count, and meeting length. Available once status is `done`.

meeting_cost_usd.title: Meeting Cost Usd
meeting_cost_usd.description: Estimated meeting cost in USD (attendee_count × avg_hourly_rate_usd × duration_hours). Only populated when `avg_hourly_rate_usd` was provided at bot creation.

pii_detected.title: Pii Detected
pii_detected.description: True if PII was detected in the transcript (only relevant when `pii_redaction=true`).

detected_decisions.title: Detected Decisions
detected_decisions.description: Decision and action moments detected during the meeting (only populated when `enable_decision_detection=true`). Each item: {kind: 'decision'|'action', text, speaker, timestamp}.

related_meetings.title: Related Meetings
related_meetings.description: Semantically related past meetings retrieved for this bot (only populated when `enable_cross_meeting_memory=true`).

total.title: Total
total.description: Total number of bots in memory (24-hour window).

active.title: Active
active.description: Number of bots currently in an active state (ready/scheduled/queued/joining/in_call/call_ended).

done.title: Done
done.description: Number of bots that completed successfully.

error.title: Error
error.description: Number of bots that ended in an error state.

by_status.title: By Status
by_status.description: Count of bots broken down by each status string.

BotSummary.title: BotSummary
BotSummary.description: Lightweight representation returned by the list endpoint (no transcript/analysis).

ical_url.title: Ical Url
ical_url.description: Publicly accessible iCal (.ics) URL, e.g. a Google Calendar private address or Outlook subscription link.

bot_name.title: Bot Name
bot_name.description: Custom bot display name for meetings from this calendar. Defaults to account bot name.

auto_record.title: Auto Record
auto_record.description: Automatically join and record detected meetings.

trigger.title: Trigger
trigger.description: Case-insensitive prefix that activates a Q&A reply (e.g. '@bot', '/ask').

reply_via.title: Reply Via
reply_via.description: How the bot should deliver answers.

rate_limit_seconds.title: Rate Limit Seconds
rate_limit_seconds.description: Minimum seconds between Q&A replies (0 disables the throttle).

text.title: Text
text.description: Text to post to the meeting chat.

ChatRequest.title: ChatRequest
ChatRequest.description: Post a message into the live meeting's chat panel.

task_id.title: Task Id
task_id.description: Opaque id for the queued chat-post task.

amount_usd.title: Amount Usd
amount_usd.description: Top-up amount in whole USD. Must be one of the values configured in `STRIPE_TOP_UP_AMOUNTS` (default: 10, 25, 50, 100).

success_url.title: Success Url
success_url.description: URL to redirect to after a successful payment. Defaults to `{base_url}/dashboard?payment=success`.

cancel_url.title: Cancel Url
cancel_url.description: URL to redirect to if the user cancels the payment. Defaults to `{base_url}/topup?payment=cancelled`.

checkout_url.title: Checkout Url
checkout_url.description: Redirect the user to this URL to complete payment.

amount_usd.title: Amount Usd
amount_usd.description: Amount that will be credited after successful payment.

metrics.title: Metrics
metrics.description: Which signals the coaching engine should track and emit tips for.

nudge_interval_seconds.title: Nudge Interval Seconds
nudge_interval_seconds.description: Minimum seconds between coaching tips per metric.

host_speaker_name.title: Host Speaker Name
host_speaker_name.description: Display name of the participant being coached. When omitted, the first non-bot participant is treated as the host.

deliver_via.title: Deliver Via
deliver_via.description: Where to push coaching tips. `sse` is private to the host UI; `webhook` fans out to subscribers.

name.title: Name
name.description: Human-readable label for this API key (e.g. 'Production', 'CI/CD').

lookback_days.title: Lookback Days
lookback_days.description: How far back to search for related past meetings.

max_meetings.title: Max Meetings
max_meetings.description: Maximum number of past meetings to surface as context.

workspace_scope.title: Workspace Scope
workspace_scope.description: Scope of the memory pool. `workspace` requires `workspace_id` to be set on the bot.

inject_into_analysis.title: Inject Into Analysis
inject_into_analysis.description: When true, related-meeting summaries are injected into the post-meeting analysis prompt.

config.title: Config
config.description: Integration config dict.  **Slack:** `{webhook_url: str}`.  **Notion:** `{api_token: str, database_id: str}`.

webhook_url.title: Webhook Url
webhook_url.description: Optional webhook URL to notify in addition to global webhooks.

keywords.title: Keywords
keywords.description: List of keywords/phrases to watch for (case-insensitive).

webhook_url.title: Webhook Url
webhook_url.description: Optional additional webhook URL to POST the alert to.

access_token.title: Access Token
access_token.description: JWT token for web UI session (valid for `JWT_EXPIRE_HOURS` hours).

LoginResponse.title: LoginResponse
LoginResponse.description:
JWT access token for authenticating web UI sessions.

**Note:** This token is intended for the browser-based web UI only.
For API calls, use your `sk_live_...` API key as a Bearer token instead.

email.title: Email
email.description: Email of the account to credit.

amount_usd.title: Amount Usd
amount_usd.description: Amount in USD to credit (must be positive).

note.title: Note
note.description: Optional admin note recorded in the transaction description.

tool.title: Tool
tool.description: Name of the tool to execute.

notify_on_done.title: Notify On Done
notify_on_done.description: Send email when a meeting recording is ready.

notify_email.title: Notify Email
notify_email.description: Override email address for notifications. Defaults to account email if omitted.

plan.title: Plan
plan.description: Current subscription plan: free | starter | pro | business.

monthly_bots_used.title: Monthly Bots Used
monthly_bots_used.description: Bots run in the current billing period.

monthly_limit.title: Monthly Limit
monthly_limit.description: Monthly bot limit for this plan (-1 = unlimited).

email.title: Email
email.description: Email address for the new account.

key_name.title: Key Name
key_name.description: Label for the first API key generated with your account.

account_type.title: Account Type
account_type.description: Account type: `personal` (default) for individual users, or `business` for platforms integrating JustHereToListen.io on behalf of multiple end-users. Business accounts can use the `X-Sub-User` header to isolate data per end-user.

api_key.title: Api Key
api_key.description: Your first API key (`sk_live_...`). Include it on every API request as: `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>`.

message.title: Message
message.description: Human-readable instructions for using the API key.

example.message: Account created. Use the api_key as your Bearer token: Authorization: Bearer <api_key>

from_block.title: From Block
from_block.description: Block number to start rescanning from.

note.title: Note
note.description: Optional note describing the resolution action taken.

rpc_url.title: Rpc Url
rpc_url.description: Ethereum JSON-RPC endpoint URL (Infura, Alchemy, QuickNode, etc.).

rpc_url_set.title: Rpc Url Set
rpc_url_set.description: True if an RPC URL is now configured.

source.title: Source
source.description: 'env' if from environment variable, 'db' if set via admin panel.

text.title: Text
text.description: Text to speak aloud in the meeting.

voice.title: Voice
voice.description: TTS provider. `gemini` — Google Gemini TTS (natural, ~1–2 s). `edge` — Microsoft Edge TTS (faster, ~300–500 ms, slightly robotic).

interrupt.title: Interrupt
interrupt.description: If true and the bot is already speaking, cancel the in-flight speech and jump ahead. If false (default) this call queues behind the current speech.

SayRequest.title: SayRequest
SayRequest.description: Speak arbitrary text in the live meeting via TTS + virtual microphone.

task_id.title: Task Id
task_id.description: Opaque id for the queued speak task. Useful for logging.

interrupted_previous.title: Interrupted Previous
interrupted_previous.description: True when interrupt=true cancelled an already-running speak task.

include_sentiment.title: Include Sentiment
include_sentiment.description: When true, run a lightweight sentiment pass on each window (extra AI cost).

include_interruptions.title: Include Interruptions
include_interruptions.description: Detect interruption events (speaker A starts within 1.5s of speaker B finishing).

renames.title: Renames
renames.description: Map of original speaker label → new display name. E.g. {'Speaker 1': 'Alice'}.

checkout_url.title: Checkout Url
checkout_url.description: Redirect the user to this URL.

expires_in_hours.title: Expires In Hours
expires_in_hours.description: Hours until the key expires. Set to null for no expiry. Defaults to 72 hours.

key.title: Key
key.description: Full test key — shown once. Use as Bearer token for sandbox mode.

amount_usd.title: Amount Usd
amount_usd.description: Amount in USD. Positive = credits added (top-up). Negative = credits deducted (bot usage).

type.title: Type
type.description:
Transaction type. One of:
- `stripe_topup` — credits added via Stripe card payment
- `usdc_topup` — credits added via USDC deposit
- `bot_usage` — credits deducted on bot completion (raw AI cost × `CREDIT_MARKUP`)

reference_id.title: Reference Id
reference_id.description: External reference. For `stripe_topup`: Stripe session ID. For `usdc_topup`: Ethereum transaction hash. For `bot_usage`: bot UUID.

created_at.title: Created At
created_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the transaction was recorded.

from_address.title: From Address
from_address.description: Sender's Ethereum address (unrecognized — not registered on any account).

to_address.title: To Address
to_address.description: Platform wallet address that received the USDC.

block_number.title: Block Number
block_number.description: Ethereum block number of the transfer.

detected_at.title: Detected At
detected_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the monitor detected the transfer.

resolved.title: Resolved
resolved.description: True if an admin has manually credited the account.

deposit_address.title: Deposit Address
deposit_address.description: The Ethereum address to send USDC to (platform collection wallet or per-user HD address).

your_wallet.title: Your Wallet
your_wallet.description: Your registered sending wallet address. If using the platform wallet, you MUST send from this address so the system can identify you.

contract.title: Contract
contract.description: USDC ERC-20 token contract address on Ethereum mainnet.

example.note: Send USDC from your registered wallet only. Credits are added automatically within ~1 minute.

ValidateMeetingUrlRequest.title: ValidateMeetingUrlRequest
ValidateMeetingUrlRequest.description: Request body for pre-flight meeting URL validation.

valid.title: Valid
valid.description: Whether the URL is a supported meeting platform.

supported.title: Supported
supported.description: Whether the platform supports real recording (not demo mode).

error_code.title: Error Code
error_code.description: Machine-readable error code if the URL is invalid.

wallet_address.title: Wallet Address
wallet_address.description: Your Ethereum wallet address (0x..., 42 characters). USDC sent from this address to the platform wallet will be credited to your account.

wallet_address.title: Wallet Address
wallet_address.description: Ethereum address (0x..., 42 characters) where users send USDC.

WebhookUpdate.title: WebhookUpdate
WebhookUpdate.description: Partial update for a registered webhook.

slug.title: Slug
slug.description: URL-safe identifier (auto-generated from name if omitted).

account_id.title: Account Id
account_id.description: Account ID of the member to add.

instructions.title: Instructions
instructions.description: Replacement list of agentic instructions. Each item: {instruction, trigger, interval_seconds?, speak?, max_invocations?}

autonomy.title: Autonomy
autonomy.description: Optional autonomy level: off | low | medium | high.

reply_via.title: Reply Via
reply_via.description: chat | voice | both. Defaults to bot config.

expires_in_hours.title: Expires In Hours
expires_in_hours.description: Number of hours the share link should remain valid. Omit to keep the previous behaviour (no expiry).

_ShareLinkRequest.title: _ShareLinkRequest
_ShareLinkRequest.description: Optional request body for share-link creation.

wallet_address.title: Wallet Address
wallet_address.description: The platform USDC collection wallet address (ERC-20). Null if not set.

wallet_address.title: Wallet Address
wallet_address.description: Your registered wallet address, or null if not set.

detail.description: Human-readable error message, or a list of Pydantic validation errors.

error_code.description: Stable machine-readable error code. SDK generators emit a typed enum from these values so callers can branch on the code without string comparison.

retryable.description: Whether the caller should retry the same request after a backoff.

incident_id.description: Set on 5xx responses for support tracing.

WebhookEvent.description: Webhook event names emitted by the platform.

WebhookPayload.description: Body POSTed to your `webhook_url`. Signed with HMAC-SHA256 in the `X-MeetingBot-Signature` header (format: `sha256=<hex>`). `X-MeetingBot-Timestamp` carries the unix-seconds delivery time.

delivery_id.description: Unique ID for this delivery attempt.

timestamp.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp the event was emitted.

bot_id.description: Bot the event relates to (omitted for `bot.test`).

data.description: Event-specific payload. For `bot.done` this includes the full BotResponse; for `bot.live_transcript` it carries `{entry: {speaker, text, source, timestamp}}`; for `bot.live_chat_message` it carries an additional `message_id`.

BearerAuth.description: Per-user API key (`sk_live_…` for production, `sk_test_…` for sandbox), or a JWT issued by `/api/v1/auth/login`. Pass as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

SubUserHeader.name: X-Sub-User
SubUserHeader.description: Business-account multi-tenant scope. Optional. When set, all data is scoped to that opaque sub-user identifier. Different sub-users on the same account cannot see each other's bots.

IdempotencyKeyHeader.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKeyHeader.description: Optional idempotency key for `POST /api/v1/bot`. A second request with the same key within `IDEMPOTENCY_TTL_HOURS` returns the original bot rather than creating a duplicate.

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: Requests remaining in the current rate-limit window.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: Unix epoch seconds at which the current window resets.

Retry-After.description: Seconds the caller should wait before retrying. Honour this on 429 and 503 responses; it overrides any backoff your client computes.
